reasoning: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.

concerns.analysis: The output contains a hallucinated URL in the 'How to use: Copilot in Teams for chat' section (page 23). The URL 'https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/category/microsoft365copilot/blog/microsoft365copilotblog' is inserted into the instruction table for the Copilot icon. This specific link does not appear anywhere in the source PDF text, screenshots, or resource lists. Other links like 'https://aka.ms/NCDIT' are correctly preserved.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source document on page 23 contains no URLs. Page 56 contains related links but does not include this specific blog URL.
concerns.location: Section 'How to use: Copilot in Teams for chat', page 23, element index 3 (table cell)

concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains 58 pages of instructional content. The output JSON only converts a small fraction of these pages (approximately 10 pages). The JSON explicitly notes that '39 sections omitted for token budget'. Specifically, pages 5, 7-22, and 25-56 are entirely absent from the conversion output, representing a loss of roughly 80% of the document's content.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Entire pages of content, such as 'Copilot in Teams Overview' (pages 7-18), 'Recap meetings' examples (pages 29-32), and 'Additional Resources' (page 56).
concerns.location: Between Slide 7 and 'How to use...' sections, and after 'How to use...' sections.

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is broken as it lacks an H1 tag for the document title. The title 'Copilot in Teams' on Slide 1 is assigned a level 2 heading. Subsequent major section titles are also level 2, and subsections (like 'Sample use cases' on Slide 23) are level 3. Standard accessibility practices require the document title to be H1.
concerns.location: Slide 1: Copilot in Teams, page 1, element index 0

concerns.analysis: Significant meaningful content is missing in the form of UI screenshots. Throughout the slide deck, Microsoft Teams interface screenshots are used to illustrate how to use Copilot features, often including red boxes, arrows, and numbered callouts (e.g., pages 10, 13, 20, 23, 31, 36, 41, 46, 51, 53). In the HTML output, the vast majority of these instructional screenshots are absent. For example, Slide 53 in the PDF contains a large graphic showing the 'Facilitator' feature with callouts for 'Ste
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 53 of the PDF displays an instructional screenshot of the Teams 'Facilitator' feature with red callouts for steps and notes.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML screenshot shows the heading 'New Feature (forthcoming! Not yet in GCC)' immediately followed by 'Tips & Tricks' with no image in between.
concerns.location: Slide 53 / element following the 'New Feature' heading

concerns.analysis: On Slide 3 (labeled as Slide 4 in the JSON section title), the bulleted items describing prompt types ('creating,', 'summarizing,', 'reviewing,', 'or researching.') are merged into a single paragraph string instead of being preserved as a list structure. This loses the structural meaning present in the source.
concerns.location: Slide 4: The art and science of prompting (JSON Index 2), page 3

concerns.analysis: On Slide 23 (index 20), the output contains a fabricated link ('https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/category/microsoft365copilot/blog/microsoft365copilotblog') associated with a placeholder dash. This URL does not appear anywhere on Page 23 of the source document.
concerns.location: How to use: Copilot in Teams for chat (JSON Index 20), page 23, table element
